problem.title: Product of Array Except Self
problem.description: Given an integer array nums, return an array answer such that answer[i] is equal to the product of all the elements of nums except nums[i].

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Two Pass
solutions.description: In the first pass, fill result[i] with the product of all elements to the left of index i. In the second pass, sweep right-to-left, multiplying each result[i] by a running right-product. This avoids division and uses the output array itself as the only extra space.
solutions.code_python:
from typing import List

def productExceptSelf(nums: List[int]) -> List[int]:
    n = len(nums)
    result = [1] * n

    # First pass: fill result[i] with product of all elements to the left
    for i in range(1, n):
        result[i] = result[i - 1] * nums[i - 1]

    # Second pass: multiply by running right product
    right = 1
    for i in range(n - 1, -1, -1):
        result[i] *= right
        right *= nums[i]

    return result
